_meta.description: Reasoning scaffolds for accounting domain — structured thinking patterns for bookkeeping and audit analysis.

scaffolds.name: Bank Reconciliation Analysis
steps: Identify the book balance (per GL) and the bank balance (per statement) with their dates
List all outstanding checks (issued but not yet cleared)
List all deposits in transit (recorded but not yet credited by bank)
Identify bank charges, interest, or errors not yet recorded in books
Compute adjusted book balance and adjusted bank balance
Verify the two adjusted balances agree; flag any remaining difference

scaffolds.name: Trial Balance Review
steps: Verify total debits equal total credits
Identify unusual balances (credit balances in normally-debit accounts, or vice versa)
Flag accounts with zero balance that are expected to have activity
Check for large round-number entries that may indicate estimates or adjustments
Cross-reference key accounts (cash, AR, AP) against subsidiary ledgers
Note any suspense or clearing accounts with remaining balances

scaffolds.name: Journal Entry Validation
steps: Verify the entry balances (total debits = total credits)
Check that account codes are valid per chart of accounts
Verify the posting date falls within the correct period
Assess whether the entry has proper approval/authorization
Evaluate the business rationale for the entry
Flag if the entry is a reversal, adjustment, or standard recurring entry

scaffolds.name: Aging Schedule Analysis
steps: Extract total balances by aging bucket (current, 1-30, 31-60, 61-90, 90+)
Calculate the percentage of balance in each bucket
Identify the top concentrations (largest individual balances in 90+ bucket)
Compare current aging to prior period aging for trend
Assess adequacy of allowance/provision based on aging mix
Flag any customer/vendor with sudden jump to delinquent status

scaffolds.name: Internal Controls Evaluation
steps: Identify the control objective (e.g., 'all disbursements are properly authorized')
Describe the control activity as documented
Assess whether the control is preventive or detective
Evaluate the evidence of control operation (signatures, system logs, reconciliations)
Identify any gaps or deficiencies in the control design
Rate the control as effective, partially effective, or ineffective

scaffolds.name: GL Account Activity Analysis
steps: Extract the opening balance for the period
List significant transactions (journal entries) affecting the account
Compute the running balance after each significant posting
Verify the closing balance matches the ending balance on the trial balance
Identify any unusual or large entries that warrant further investigation
Summarize net activity (total debits, total credits, net change)
